# servicenow_transform_map (Resource)

`servicenow_transform_map` manages a transform map (sys_transform_map) within ServiceNow for import data set field mapping.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a transform map (sys_transform_map) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_transform_map" "example" {
  name                     = "Example User Import"
  source_table             = "u_user_import"
  target_table             = "sys_user"
  active                   = true
  run_business_rules       = true
  enforce_mandatory_fields = "no"
  copy_empty_fields        = false
  order                    = 100
  description              = "Imports staged user rows into sys_user."
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the transform map.
- `source_table` (String) Source table (typically an import set staging table).
- `target_table` (String) Target table that the transform map will write records to.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this transform map is enabled.
- `copy_empty_fields` (Boolean) If true, copy empty source values to the target record.
- `enforce_mandatory_fields` (String) Mandatory field enforcement setting (No, error, warn).
- `order` (Number) Order in which the map is executed.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `run_business_rules` (Boolean) If true, run business rules on the target table during transformation.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script` (String) Optional transform script to execute during transformation. ServiceNow seeds a default function body when omitted.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_transform_map.example <sys_id>
```
